questions.name: ellipsis_conj_objc
questions.question: Find all ellipsis clauses that start with a conjunction and an object
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[clause typ=Ellp
   [phrase first function=Conj]
   ..
   [phrase function=Objc]
]
GO
questions.notes: the motivating example; first + .. semantics per research

questions.name: niphal_verbs
questions.question: Find all Niphal verbs in the Hebrew Bible
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[word sp=verb AND vs=nif
   GET g_word_utf8, gloss
]
GO
questions.notes: word-level filter on sp (part of speech) and vs (verbal stem); both enum features unquoted

questions.name: bara_occurrences
questions.question: Where does the verb bara (create) occur in the text?
questions.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [verse GET book, chapter, verse [word lex='BR>[' GET g_word_utf8, gloss]] GO
questions.notes: where-question; the verse wrap GETs book/chapter/verse so each hit shows its location (the reference pattern)

questions.name: object_phrases
questions.question: Find all object phrases in the Hebrew Bible
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[phrase function=Objc
   GET function
]
GO
questions.notes: single-level phrase filter; function is enum on phrase, unquoted

questions.name: imperative_verbs
questions.question: Find all imperative verb forms
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[word sp=verb AND vt=impv
   GET g_word_utf8, gloss
]
GO
questions.notes: vt (verbal tense) is enum; impv = imperative; sp and vt both unquoted

questions.name: nominal_clause_with_subject
questions.question: Find nominal clauses that contain a subject phrase
questions.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=NmCl [phrase function=Subj]] GO
questions.notes: nominal (verbless) clause with an explicit subject; reformulated from a Pred-based query that returned 0 (verbless clauses use PreC, not Pred)

questions.name: construct_chain_nouns
questions.question: Find noun phrases that contain a construct-chain (regens-rectum) relationship
questions.notes: subphrase.rela values are lowercase in BHSA; rec = regens (construct chain); phrase.typ NP = nominal phrase

questions.name: feminine_plural_nouns
questions.question: Find all feminine plural nouns
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[word sp=subs AND gn=f AND nu=pl
   GET g_word_utf8, gloss
]
GO
questions.notes: sp=subs (noun/substantive), gn=f (feminine), nu=pl (plural); all enum features unquoted

questions.name: wayyiqtol_clause_subject
questions.question: Find wayyiqtol clauses that have a subject phrase
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[clause typ=WayX
   [phrase function=Subj
      GET function
   ]
]
GO
questions.notes: WayX = Wayyiqtol-X clause (X-slot filled, including subject); combination of clause type filter with nested phrase function

questions.name: verse_proper_nouns_genesis
questions.question: Where do proper nouns occur in Genesis?
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[verse book=Genesis
   [word sp=nmpr
      GET g_word_utf8, gloss
   ]
]
GO
questions.notes: verse-nest pattern filtered by book (enum on verse); sp=nmpr = proper noun; both enum features unquoted

questions.name: verbal_clause_pred_obj
questions.question: Find verbal clauses where the predicate is followed by an object phrase
questions.mql:
SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE
[clause kind=VC
   [phrase function=Pred]
   ..
   [phrase function=Objc]
]
GO
questions.notes: kind=VC targets all verbal clause subtypes; .. separates Pred and Objc to allow intervening phrases; clause.kind is enum unquoted
